preamble: The shift from the bilabial stop [b] to the alveolar/dental stop [d] is known as coronalization. It is relatively rare as a primary sound change but occurs through intermediate stages of palatalization (where [b] before a front vowel develops a coronal articulation) or through acoustic confusion in rapid speech.

examples.note: Labial consonants often undergo palatalization/coronalization in passive morphological constructions.
Coronal substitution is a common developmental stage where bilabials are replaced by the more central alveolar stops.
